title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
The manager of a company has asked employees to write a report about working conditions in the office. Your report should describe the current working environment, explain what employees find most challenging, and suggest improvements that could increase productivity and job satisfaction.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Working Conditions at Meridian Solutions

Introduction
This report was written following a survey of 45 employees and aims to assess current working conditions and suggest practical improvements.

Current Working Environment
The majority of employees are satisfied with their basic facilities, including the kitchen and meeting rooms. However, several concerns were raised. The open-plan office creates constant noise, making it difficult to concentrate, particularly for those working on complex tasks. Furthermore, 60% of respondents stated they regularly work beyond their contracted hours due to unrealistic deadlines.

Main Challenges
The most significant issues identified are noise levels, excessive workload, and a lack of flexible working options. Many employees expressed a strong desire for the option to work from home at least two days per week.

Recommendations
The company should create a designated quiet zone for focused work. Additionally, introducing flexible working hours and reviewing project timelines would help prevent burnout. A monthly team wellbeing meeting would also give employees a regular opportunity to raise concerns.

Conclusion
Addressing these points would significantly boost both morale and productivity across the company.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
You have seen a job advertisement for a part-time position at an international company. Write an email to the Human Resources department expressing your interest in the position, explaining why you would be suitable, and asking for further details about the role.

Write your email.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear HR Department,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the part-time Customer Relations Assistant position advertised on your website last Tuesday. I believe I have the skills and experience needed to contribute effectively to your team.

I am currently in my final year of a Business Studies degree and have eighteen months of experience working part-time at a local retail company. In this role, I developed excellent communication skills and learned how to handle customer complaints professionally and efficiently. I also have a good level of written and spoken English, which I understand is essential for this position.

I am particularly drawn to your company because of its reputation for innovation and its commitment to employee development. I am eager to learn from experienced professionals in an international environment.

Could you please send me more details about the specific responsibilities of the role, the working hours, and the application deadline? I would also be grateful to know whether the position involves any travel.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Yours sincerely,
Tom Adesanya

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language careers magazine:

'We are looking for articles about the best qualities an employer should have. What do you think makes a great boss or manager? Share your ideas with our readers.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
What Makes a Great Manager?

Most people have experienced both good and bad managers during their working lives. The difference between the two can have a dramatic effect on how much you enjoy your job and how well you perform. So what separates a truly great manager from an average one?

In my view, the most important quality is the ability to listen. A great manager takes the time to understand their employees' ideas, concerns, and ambitions. They do not simply issue instructions from above — they engage in genuine two-way communication.

Equally important is the ability to inspire and motivate. People naturally work harder when they feel appreciated and when they believe their work has purpose. A good manager regularly recognises effort and celebrates team achievements.

Finally, a great manager leads by example. They would never ask their team to do something they would not do themselves. This consistency builds trust and respect — two qualities that are essential in any workplace.

In summary, the best managers combine strong communication, genuine empathy, and personal integrity. These are the qualities that create loyal, motivated, and high-performing teams.
